jsonLdWeb.name: Deck Board Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate deck boards, linear feet, screws, and joists for any deck project.

mainEntity.name: How many deck boards do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Divide the deck width by the board width plus gap, then multiply by the number of boards needed to span the deck length. For a 12×16 ft deck with 5.5" boards and 1/8" gap: 192" ÷ 5.625" = 34.1 → 35 rows. If using 16 ft boards, you need 35 boards. If using shorter boards, calculate the number needed per row.

mainEntity.name: What gap should I leave between deck boards?

acceptedAnswer.text: For wood decking, leave 1/8" to 1/4" between boards for drainage and expansion. Pressure-treated lumber installed wet may need less initial gap as it shrinks while drying. Composite decking typically requires 1/8" side gap and 1/8" to 3/16" end gap per manufacturer specifications.

mainEntity.name: How many screws do I need for decking?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plan on 2 screws per board per joist. For a deck with joists at 16" OC, a 16 ft long board crosses about 13 joists, needing 26 screws. A rough estimate is 350 screws per 100 square feet of decking at 16" joist spacing. A 5 lb box of 2.5" deck screws contains roughly 350 screws.

mainEntity.name: What is the standard deck board width?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard deck board widths: 5/4×6 lumber has a 5.5" actual face width, 2×6 is also 5.5", and 2×4 is 3.5". Composite boards are typically 5.5" wide. Wider boards cover area faster but are more prone to cupping. Most residential decks use 5.5" boards.

mainEntity.name: How far apart should deck joists be?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard joist spacing is 16" on-center for most wood and composite decking. Some composite manufacturers allow 12" OC for diagonal installations. 24" OC is only suitable for heavy timber decking (2" thick). Always check the decking manufacturer's requirements for maximum joist spacing.

mainEntity.name: How much waste should I plan for?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plan for 10-15% waste for a rectangular deck with boards running parallel to the longest side. Diagonal patterns need 15-20% extra. Complex shapes with lots of cuts may need 20%+. If using random-length boards, plan for 5-10% waste from end-matching.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Deck Boards

Deck board calculations determine how many boards cover the deck surface, accounting for the gap between each board.

Rows = Deck Width ÷ (Board Width + Gap)
  • Board rows = deck width in inches ÷ (board face width + gap)
  • Boards per row = ceiling of (deck length ÷ board length)
  • Total boards = rows × boards per row × (1 + waste %)
  • Linear feet = total boards × board length
  • Screws = 2 per board per joist crossing
  • Joists = (deck length in inches ÷ joist spacing) + 1
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many deck boards do I need?

Divide the deck width (in inches) by the board width plus gap, then multiply by the number of boards needed to span the length. For a 12×16 ft deck with 5.5" boards and 1/8" gap: 144" ÷ 5.625" = 25.6 → 26 rows of 16 ft boards.

What gap between deck boards?

Use 1/8" to 1/4" between boards for drainage and expansion. Pressure-treated lumber installed wet needs less initial gap. Composite decking typically requires 1/8" side gap per manufacturer specifications.

How many screws for decking?

Use 2 screws per board per joist. Roughly 350 screws per 100 sq ft at 16" joist spacing. A 5 lb box of 2.5" deck screws has about 350 screws.

What is standard deck board width?

Most residential decks use 5.5" wide boards (5/4×6 lumber or composite). Also available: 3.5" (2×4) for narrow patterns and 7.25" (2×8) for wide plank looks.

How far apart should deck joists be?

Standard is 16" OC for most wood and composite. Some composites require 12" OC for diagonal patterns. Only heavy 2" timber decking is suitable for 24" OC joist spacing.

How much waste should I plan for?

10-15% for rectangular decks with parallel boards. 15-20% for diagonal patterns. 20%+ for complex shapes with many cuts.
